tools.name: get_rosetta_info
tools.description: Get comprehensive Rosetta installation info including available score functions, movers, filters, selectors, task operations, parameters, and command-line options. Use this first to understand what Rosetta components are available. For live PyRosetta API details, use pyrosetta_introspect.

tools.name: get_rosetta_help
tools.description: Get help for any Rosetta topic, mover, filter, or concept. Accepts general topics (score_functions, movers, filters, xml, parameters) or specific names (FastRelax, Ddg, ChainSelector). Auto-fetches live documentation when available.

tools.name: validate_xml
tools.description: Validate a RosettaScripts XML protocol. Checks XML syntax and optionally validates element names against the Rosetta XSD schema. Use before run_rosetta_scripts to catch errors early.

validate_against_schema.description: If true, also check element names against the cached Rosetta XSD schema (default: false)

tools.name: run_rosetta_scripts
tools.description: Run a RosettaScripts XML protocol on an input PDB file. Use when executing Rosetta protocols. Requires rosetta_scripts binary.

exe_path.description: Path to rosetta_scripts executable (optional if on PATH)

tools.name: pyrosetta_score
tools.description: Score a PDB file using PyRosetta. Returns total energy in REU. Use to evaluate structure quality or compare designs. Optionally returns per-residue energy breakdown.

per_residue.description: If true, include per-residue energy breakdown (default: false)

tools.name: pyrosetta_introspect
tools.description: Search PyRosetta API classes (movers, filters, selectors, task operations) and return docs and signatures. Use to discover available PyRosetta classes or get constructor details.

tools.name: rosetta_scripts_schema
tools.description: Generate and cache the RosettaScripts XML schema (XSD). Optionally extract element names. Use to get the authoritative list of valid XML elements.

extract_elements.description: If true, return a list of element names

tools.name: get_cached_docs
tools.description: Search locally cached Rosetta CLI docs for a keyword. Auto-caches on first use. Use to look up command-line flags or parser info.

max_lines.description: Max number of lines to return (default 200)

tools.name: python_env_info
tools.description: Get Python executable path, version, and pip package list. Use to diagnose environment issues or verify package installations.

tools.name: check_pyrosetta
tools.description: Check if PyRosetta is importable in the current environment. Use before PyRosetta-dependent tools to verify availability.

tools.name: install_pyrosetta_installer
tools.description: Install PyRosetta using the pyrosetta-installer package. Takes 10-30 minutes. Use when PyRosetta is not available and needed for scoring or design.

tools.name: find_rosetta_scripts
tools.description: Resolve the rosetta_scripts executable path by checking exe_path, ROSETTA_BIN env, common directories, and PATH. Use to verify Rosetta is installed.

tools.name: xml_to_pyrosetta
tools.description: Translate RosettaScripts XML to equivalent PyRosetta Python code. Supports 37 element types including movers, filters, selectors, and task operations. Use when converting XML protocols to Python.

include_comments.description: Include detailed comments in output (default: true)

tools.name: search_rosetta_web_docs
tools.description: Search online Rosetta documentation at rosettacommons.org. Use when you need docs for a specific Rosetta feature.

tools.name: get_rosetta_web_doc
tools.description: Fetch and extract text from a specific Rosetta docs URL. Use after search_rosetta_web_docs to read a documentation page.

url.description: Full URL to a Rosetta docs page

max_chars.description: Max characters of cleaned text to return (default 4000)

tools.name: rosetta_to_biotite
tools.description: ALWAYS use this tool when asked about Rosetta vs Biotite equivalents. Returns the Biotite equivalent of a Rosetta/PyRosetta function with working example code. Covers: structure I/O, SASA, RMSD, superimposition, secondary structure, distances, angles, contacts, hydrogen bonds, B-factors, interface analysis, database access, and residue selection.

tools.name: biotite_to_rosetta
tools.description: ALWAYS use this tool when asked about Biotite vs Rosetta equivalents. Returns the Rosetta/PyRosetta equivalent of a Biotite function with working example code. Covers: structure I/O, SASA, RMSD, superimposition, secondary structure, distances, angles, contacts, hydrogen bonds, B-factors, and residue selection.

tools.name: translate_rosetta_script_to_biotite
tools.description: ALWAYS use this tool when asked to convert or translate Rosetta code to Biotite. Translates RosettaScripts XML or PyRosetta code to Biotite Python code. Analysis operations are translated; design/optimization are flagged as Rosetta-only.

code.description: RosettaScripts XML content or PyRosetta Python code to translate

include_comments.description: Include explanatory comments in output (default: true)

findings.title: 'pyrosetta_introspect' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: 'rosetta_scripts_schema' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: 'install_pyrosetta_installer' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: 'find_rosetta_scripts' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'get_rosetta_help.topic'
findings.description: Payload was reflected verbatim in the response without sanitization (field classification: generic_string).
findings.evidence:
No detailed help found for "<script>alert(1)</script>". Try:
- pyrosetta_introspect with query="<script>alert(1)</script>" for live API details
- get_rosetta_web_doc with a specific rosettacommons.org URL
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Protocol crash — oversized method name caused transport failure
findings.description: The server failed to respond to a request with an 8192-byte method name. A robust server should return a JSON-RPC error, not disconnect or crash.
findings.remediation: Add input length validation at the JSON-RPC parsing layer. Return -32700 (parse error) or -32600 (invalid request) for malformed inputs.

findings.title: Protocol crash — deeply nested params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request with 50-level nested params caused the server to disconnect or crash. Deep nesting can cause stack overflows or excessive memory use in recursive JSON parsers.
findings.remediation: Limit JSON object nesting depth at parse time (recommended: ≤32 levels). Return -32700 (Parse error) for inputs exceeding the depth limit.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 15 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.
